item.title: SENIOR AI Engineer Tech Stack [2026]
item.description:
Vishakha Sadhwani: SENIOR AI Engineer Tech Stack [2026]
Learning Resources: https://gdrive.openinapp.co/v2gcw
AI systems don’t run on models alone.
→ How do you deploy models on GPUs? → How do you scale inference across clusters? → How do you automate infra without breaking production?
The gap isn’t AI knowledge. It’s infrastructure understanding.
And these are the tools that actually bridge that gap 👇
1. Kubernetes (The Foundation) The operating system of modern AI. Runs your models, manages GPU clusters, keeps systems scalable.
2. Helm + Argo CD (The Pipeline) The GitOps standard. Automates deployments and keeps your infra in sync with code.
3. Claude Code (The Workflow) AI inside your terminal. Helps you debug, architect, and move faster. If you want to become an AI Solutions Architect, This is the stack that actually matters.
If you build strength here, you position yourself for cloud, platform, and AI infrastructure roles.

item.title: FULL AIOps Project [Step-By-Step] | DevOps Hands-On Project Demo | Part 4 | Vishakha Sadhwani
item.description:
Check out UptimeRobot: https://uptimerobot.com/vishakha Use code "Vishakha" at checkout to get 10% off any paid plan.
GitHub Repo: https://github.com/vishakhasadhwani/devops-ai-playbook
Complete Playlist: https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLXkUFcIv0_b7rzZe0o_2-GOS2qn5-0OQy
This is Part 4 of my complete DevOps + AIOps end-to-end project series, where I’ll show you how to add an AI layer to your DevOps system and build a real AIOps assistant.
I’ve covered how to build an AI agent using AWS Bedrock, integrate it with Lambda functions for logs, metrics, and health checks, connect CloudWatch and Prometheus data, and create a chat-based interface using Streamlit to troubleshoot production issues in real time.
Watch till the end to see how an AI assistant can analyze your system, detect issues, and suggest fixes automatically.
Also, check out the previous parts of this DevOps project series if you haven't already!
CHAPTERS: 00:00 Intro 01:27 Essential Concepts 04:01 Cloud Services Overview 06:30 AIOps Architecture Walkthrough 10:44 Fluent Bit Setup (Logs to CloudWatch) 13:57 Lambda Functions Setup (Logs, Metrics, Health) 23:29 Bedrock Agent Setup 29:01 Deployment Script (deploy.sh) 33:04 Testing the AI Agent 34:32 Streamlit UI Setup 37:33 Live Demo (AIOps in Action) 40:40 Recap

item.title: 3 Types of CI/CD Pipelines EXPLAINED | What Is CI/CD Pipeline?
item.description:
In this short, I’ll break down the three most important deployment pipelines every DevOps, Cloud, or MLOps engineer should understand: Traditional CI/CD, GitOps, and MLOps pipelines.
I’ve explained how push-based CI/CD works, how GitOps uses Git as the source of truth with tools like Argo CD, and how MLOps pipelines validate data, retrain models, benchmark performance, and safely deploy AI systems.
Master all three if you want to stay relevant in this Cloud/AI era.

This is Part 3 of my complete DevOps + AI end-to-end project series, where I’ll walk you through the real implementation phase as we start building and deploying our microservices-based application using modern DevOps tools.
I’ve covered how to run the project locally with Docker Compose, provision AWS infrastructure using Terraform, trigger CI pipelines with GitHub Actions, sync deployments using Argo CD, and set up an observability stack for monitoring. Also, I’ve shown how Claude Code can support modern DevOps workflows using AI tools.
This video is ideal for DevOps engineers, cloud learners, backend developers, and anyone who wants real hands-on project experience.
Watch till the end and stay tuned for Part 4, where I’ll walk you through AIOps in practice.

item.title: The Testaments
item.description:
A new show has been added to showRSS: The Testaments.

The Testaments is based on Margaret Atwood's Booker Prize-winning novel of the same name, which was published in 2019 and takes place in the dystopian theocracy of Gilead. Years after the events of The Handmaid's Tale, The Testaments is a coming of age story that finds a new generation of young women in Gilead grappling with the bleak future that awaits them. For these young women, growing up in Gilead is all they have ever known, having no tangible memories of the outside world prior to their indoctrination into this life. Facing the prospect of being married off and living a life of servitude, they will be forced to search for allies, both new and old, to help in their fight for freedom and the life they deserve.

item.title: COMPLETE DevOps + AIOps End-To-End Project Walkthrough | Part 2 | Vishakha Sadhwani
item.description:
Complete Playlist: https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLXkUFcIv0_b7rzZe0o_2-GOS2qn5-0OQy
This is Part 2 of my complete end-to-end DevOps + AIOps project series, where I’ll break down the full architecture and workflow of the project using a real-world microservices e-commerce application.
I’ve covered how seven microservices run locally with Docker Compose, how infrastructure is provisioned on AWS using Terraform, how CI/CD works with GitHub Actions, how Argo CD enables GitOps deployments, and how Prometheus + Grafana power observability.
This video is ideal for DevOps engineers, cloud learners, backend developers, and anyone who wants to understand how modern production systems are actually built.
Watch till the end, and stay tuned for Part 3, where we will focus on hands-on implementation from scratch.

item.title: System Design Concepts CRASH COURSE For Beginners | End-To-End DevOps + AIOps Project | Part 1
item.description:
Complete Playlist: https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLXkUFcIv0_b7rzZe0o_2-GOS2qn5-0OQy
This is Part 1 of my complete end-to-end DevOps + AIOps end-to end project series, where I’ll break down the core system design principles behind modern DevOps and cloud architectures and show how they connect to the project we’ll be building.
I’ve organized these concepts into five key pillars: scalability, reliability, security, performance, and observability.
Under these pillars, I’ve covered distributed systems, microservices, APIs, service discovery, load balancing, auto scaling, Kubernetes resilience, deployment strategies, and GitOps, while explaining why each concept matters in real production environments and how it will be applied inside our AWS EKS e-commerce microservices project with CI/CD automation and an AIOps layer.
Watch till the end and stay tuned for Part 2, where I’ll break down the full architecture and workflow!
CHAPTERS: 00:00 Intro 01:18 Distributed Systems 02:57 Monolith vs Microservices 05:30 API Communication 08:36 Service Discovery 10:10 Load Balancing 13:34 High Availability 15:35 Autoscaling 17:50 Reliability with Kubernetes 19:34 Security by Design 22:27 Observability 23:49 Deployment Strategies 26:18 GitOps 28:10 Conclusion

item.title: COMPLETE DevOps + AIOps End-To-End Project | Introduction | Vishakha Sadhwani
item.description:
Complete Playlist: https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLXkUFcIv0_b7rzZe0o_2-GOS2qn5-0OQy
This video is an introduction to my very first DevOps & AI project series, where I’ll build a complete end-to-end DevOps project with AIOps integration from scratch to help you understand how AI is integrated in the DevOps workflow.
The series is divided into 4 parts:
Part - 1: System Design Foundations Part - 2: Understanding The Project Workflow Part - 3: DevOps Project Implementation Part - 4: AIOps Integration
While discussing project workflows, I’ll be covering the necessary system design foundations, Docker, Kubernetes, CI/CD pipelines, GitOps automation, cloud migration, monitoring, and AIOps use cases like log analysis, troubleshooting, and intelligent incident response, and also demo the complete project for you to see the final result!
This series is designed for students, DevOps engineers, cloud engineers, and software developers who want to move beyond tutorials and learn how real production systems are designed, deployed, and managed.
This can also serve as a really good resume project for 2026 DevOps jobs!
Leave a like, comment, and subscribe for more such videos.

Jury Duty
item.description:
A new show has been added to showRSS: Jury Duty.

Jury Duty chronicles the inner workings of an American jury trial through the eyes of one particular juror, Ronald Gladden. Gladden is unaware the entire case is fake, everyone except him is an actor, including Marsden, and everything that happens — inside the courtroom and out — is carefully planned.

item.title: Star Wars: Maul - Shadow Lord
item.description:
A new show has been added to showRSS: Star Wars: Maul - Shadow Lord.

Star Wars: Maul - Shadow Lord picks up after the events of Star Wars: The Clone Wars, for a pulpy adventure that finds Maul plotting to rebuild his criminal syndicate on a planet untouched by the Empire. There, he crosses paths with a disillusioned young Jedi Padawan who may just be the apprentice he is seeking to aid him in his relentless pursuit for revenge.

item.title: The Madison
item.description:
A new show has been added to showRSS: The Madison.

The Madison is a heartfelt study of grief and human connection following a New York City family in the Madison River valley of central Montana. It is a profound love story channeled through a deeply personal family drama about resilience and transformation. The Madison is unfolding across two distinct worlds - the beautiful landscape of Montana and the vibrant energy of Manhattan - as it examines the ties that bind families together.

item.title: 4 LEVELS Of Docker 🐳👩🏻‍💻⚙️
item.description:
Vishakha Sadhwani: 4 LEVELS Of Docker 🐳👩🏻‍💻⚙️
Learning Resources: https://gdrive.openinapp.co/12bn5
When I first started using Docker, I could run containers, build images, and follow tutorials, and I thought I was “good enough.”
But in real systems, that’s where the gaps start showing.
→ Why is your image size 2GB? → Why does one small change rebuild everything? → Why does it work locally but break in production?
That’s when I realized the importance of understanding Docker at different levels.
And moving across these levels is what actually helps you: → debug faster instead of guessing → optimize systems instead of overbuilding → understand when to use containers vs other approaches
This is the difference between “I use Docker” vs “I can design systems with it.”
If you’re serious about DevOps, this shift matters more than just learning commands.
Leave a like, comment, and subscribe for more!
Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/vishakha.sadhwani LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/in/vsadhwani/
Subscribe to my Newsletter: https://www.tech5ense.com/
-----------------------------------------------

I’m Vishakha ☁️; A Cloud Architect and DevOps enthusiast who loves turning complex cloud concepts into simple, practical insights you can actually use. You’ll find real-world projects 💻 quick explainers ☕ and honest career advice 💬; From Kubernetes and Terraform to landing roles and building your tech brand, I keep things approachable, actionable, and a little fun so learning Cloud and DevOps actually feels exciting.
If this sounds like your vibe, hit subscribe, and let’s keep growing together 🚀

item.title: Top 15 AWS Services You MUST KNOW 🚀
item.description:
Vishakha Sadhwani: Top 15 AWS Services You MUST KNOW 🚀
Watch the full video here: https://yt.openinapp.co/24nf2
When I started exploring AWS, the number of services honestly confused me. It felt like you needed to learn everything to build anything.
But over time, I realized, you don’t.
You only need a small set of core services, and more importantly, clarity on when to use what:
→ EC2 vs Lambda → S3 vs RDS vs DynamoDB → ECS vs EKS
That’s what actually makes the difference.
So, in this video, I’ve broken it down into 7 core key pillars I wish I had focused on earlier, along with projects that help you apply this in real-world scenarios.
This is the approach that helps you move from just learning AWS to actually building with it.

item.title: Ludwig
item.description:
A new show has been added to showRSS: Ludwig.

Can accidental detective John 'Ludwig' Taylor crack the biggest puzzle of his life - finding his missing brother?

item.title: Welcome to Chippendales
item.description:
A new show has been added to showRSS: Welcome to Chippendales.

A sprawling true-crime saga, Welcome to Chippendales tells the outrageous story of Somen "Steve" Banerjee, an Indian immigrant who became the unlikely founder of the world's greatest male-stripping empire - and let nothing stand in his way in the process. The series details the insane, darkly comedic, crime-ridden story behind the unique male revue that became a cultural phenomenon.

item.title: The Beauty
item.description:
A new show has been added to showRSS: The Beauty.

When international supermodels begin dying in gruesome and mysterious ways, two FBI agents are sent to Paris, Venice, Rome and New York to uncover the truth behind these deaths and end up discovering a conspiracy that threatens the future of humanity.

item.title: CIA
item.description:
A new show has been added to showRSS: CIA.

CIA centers on two unlikely partners — a fast-talking, rule-breaking loose cannon CIA case officer, and a by-the-book, seasoned and smart FBI agent who believes in the rule of law. When this odd couple are assigned to work out of CIA's New York Station, they must learn to work together to investigate cases and criminals posing threats on U.S. soil, finding that their differences may actually be their strength.

item.title: Marshals
item.description:
A new show has been added to showRSS: Marshals.

Marshals stars Luke Grimes reprising his role as Kayce Dutton. With the Yellowstone Ranch behind him, Dutton joins an elite unit of U.S. Marshals, combining his skills as a cowboy and Navy SEAL to bring range justice to Montana. Kayce and his teammates - Pete Calvin, Belle Skinner, Andrea Cruz and Miles Kittle - must balance the high psychological cost of serving as the last line of defense in the region's war on violence with their duty to their families, which for Kayce includes his son Tate and his confidants Thomas Rainwater and Mo from the Broken Rock reservation.

item.description: In theaters on March 13 is ‘Reminders of Him’, the latest adaptation of a novel by prolific romance/melodrama author Colleen Hoover, which stars Maika Monroe ('Longlegs') as a young woman looking to put her life back together.

item.title: Going Dutch
item.description:
A new show has been added to showRSS: Going Dutch.

In Going Dutch a decorated, hard-ass colonel is tasked with running the least important U.S. Army base in the world, located in the tulip-hugging, wine-chugging Netherlands.

item.title: All Elite Wrestling: Dynamite
item.description:
A new show has been added to showRSS: All Elite Wrestling: Dynamite.

WarnerMedia announced today that it is partnering with All Elite Wrestling (AEW), the new professional wrestling promotion featuring a world-class roster of diverse male and female wrestlers, giving fans a new wrestling experience for the first time in 20 years. WarnerMedia and AEW together will build this powerhouse sporting league from the ground up and will begin airing weekly matches later this year. With this league, AEW is introducing a new generation of wrestlers to fans, offering fun, gripping and authentic athletic matches that will make wrestling more accessible to a broad audience.

item.title: Heated Rivalry
item.description:
A new show has been added to showRSS: Heated Rivalry.

Two hockey players must decide if there is room in their fiercely competitive world for real love.

item.title: Scrubs (2026)
item.description:
A new show has been added to showRSS: Scrubs (2026).

Old friends JD and Turk scrub in together for the first time in a long time, learning that medicine has changed; interns have changed; but their bromance has stood the test of time.

item.title: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms
item.description:
A new show has been added to showRSS: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms.

A century before the events of Game of Thrones, two unlikely heroes wandered Westeros: a young, naive but courageous knight, Ser Duncan the Tall, and his diminutive squire, Egg. Set in an age when the Targaryen line still holds the Iron Throne and the last dragon has not yet passed from living memory, great destinies, powerful foes, and dangerous exploits await these improbable and incomparable friends.

item.title: Star Trek: Starfleet Academy
item.description:
A new show has been added to showRSS: Star Trek: Starfleet Academy.

Star Trek: Starfleet Academy is set in the 32nd century, a time period in the far-future of the Star Trek franchise where Starfleet and the United Federation of Planets are recovering from a cataclysmic event, as depicted in Star Trek: Discovery. It introduces viewers to a young group of cadets who come together to pursue a common dream of hope and optimism. Under the watchful and demanding eyes of their instructors, they discover what it takes to become Starfleet officers as they navigate blossoming friendships, explosive rivalries, first loves and a new enemy that threatens both the Academy and the Federation itself.

item.title: The Real Housewives of Salt Lake City
item.description:
A new show has been added to showRSS: The Real Housewives of Salt Lake City.

The Real Housewives of Salt Lake City takes viewers into an undiscovered world that is about so much more than religion, although the Mormon church is undeniably engrained in the culture. In the majestic mountains of Utah is a hidden social circle made up of successful women who have created their own paradise filled with luxury homes, shopping sprees and multi-million-dollar businesses and brands. With Hollywood's exclusive Sundance Film Festival in their backyards, these Type-A power-brokers are accustomed to rubbing elbows with A-listers and what party list you are on means everything. They have mastered running wildly successful enterprises by day and enjoy après ski at the finest resorts by night, all the while raising extraordinary and unconventional families. Here perfection is not an aspiration, it's a mandate. They hold themselves and each other to an extremely high standard and never hold back when things go sideways, but lord knows they are always there for each other when they need it most.

item.title: Tracker
item.description:
A new show has been added to showRSS: Tracker.

Tracker features lone-wolf survivalist Colter Shaw, who roams the country as a "reward seeker," using his expert tracking skills to help private citizens and law enforcement to solve all manner of mysteries while contending with his own fractured family.

item.title: The War Between the Land and the Sea
item.description:
A new show has been added to showRSS: The War Between the Land and the Sea.

When a fearsome and ancient species emerges from the ocean, dramatically revealing themselves to humanity, an international crisis is triggered. With the entire population at risk, UNIT step into action as the land and sea wage war.
